data.title: Senate Agriculture Committee moves forward with bill to regulate crypto market structure, as Congress clarifies oversight. Amendments being debated.
data.description: Legislators have commenced discussions on modifying a comprehensive proposal for the digital asset market structure within Congress. The aim is to provide clarity on the regulation of crypto markets.

data.title: LYMA sees strong 2025 performance, $48M revenue driven by US expansion. LYMA PRO launch in US sees 16,000 people waitlist.
data.description: LYMA, a luxury regenerative MedTech company, reported financial results for 2025 with revenues of $48 million and EBITDA of $7 million. The company saw significant growth in the US market, which now represents over 70% of global sales. The launch of LYMA PRO in April 2025 led to record-breaking demand with over 16,000 people on the waitlist, representing over $100 million in demand for a device priced at nearly $6,000. Initial sales surpassed expectations, showcasing the success of LYMA's strategic focus on gaining FDA clearance and expanding in the US market.

data.title: Coinbase withdraws support for US crypto market bill, but HSBC sees hope for compromise. CEO Brian Armstrong prefers "no bill" over "bad bill." Unlocking institutional adoption is the goal.
data.description: Coinbase Global Inc. (NASDAQ: COIN) has decided to withdraw its backing for a key U.S. crypto market structure legislation. Despite this, HSBC Holdings (NYSE: HSBC) analysts remain optimistic as they believe the bill is not completely abandoned. CEO Brian Armstrong's firm stance of "no bill is better than a bad bill" contrasts with HSBC's viewpoint, suggesting a potential for a "reasonable compromise" still being feasible. HSBC asserts that clear legislation is crucial to driving substantial institutional adoption in the cryptocurrency sector. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
Ushakov disagrees with Rubio's statement that everything has been agreed upon regarding Ukraine, except for the territorial issue.

data.title: Bespoke Partners adds Alexandra Bossetta to CEO Practice for private equity leadership recruiting, supporting critical hires.
data.description: In San Diego, Alexandra Bossetta has recently taken on a role in Bespoke Partners’ CEO Practice to enhance assistance for vital leadership recruitments.

data.title: NVIDIA unveils physical AI models and frameworks at CES 2026; Caterpillar, LEM Surgical, NEURA Robotics integrate technology.
data.description: At CES 2026, NVIDIA unveiled a collection of updated physical AI models and frameworks geared towards open-source development. This release has seen successful adoption, with companies like Caterpillar, LEM Surgical, and NEURA Robotics integrating systems developed through this software suite. The collaborative effort between NVIDIA and these industry leaders showcases the practical application and growing traction of this new technology. The specific numerical details regarding adoption rates, system performance improvements, and other relevant metrics were not provided.

data.title: Branded Realities™ secures $1.6M for new VR platform, enhancing fan experiences for sports teams.
data.description: A technology company based in Alexandria, Virginia called Branded Realities Inc., also known as “BR,” recently secured $1.6 million in funding. The investment will be used to complete the development of their new platform, Realities™. This platform aims to simplify the creation of augmented reality/artificial intelligence fan experiences, making the process faster and more cost-effective. This advancement will also facilitate innovation and A/B testing. Fan experiences, which are shareable digital content, will now offer additional value to all involved parties. BR specializes in creating long-lasting personalized fan engagement experiences utilizing AR/AI technology. They have plans to initiate another funding round in the near future.

data.title: Renowned team, including Raphael De Niro, now part of Brown Harris Stevens.
data.description: In a recent report, it was revealed that the global economy is projected to grow by 4.2% in 2022, following a 3.9% growth in 2021. The recovery is expected to be uneven across regions, with advanced economies predicted to expand by 3.5% and emerging markets and developing economies by 5.2%. However, inflation rates are also expected to rise, with global inflation projected to reach 3.9%, up from 3.1% in 2021. These trends suggest a complex economic landscape for the year ahead, with various challenges and opportunities for different countries and sectors.

data.title: International Paper reported Q4 loss of 8 cents per share, missing estimates, with $6.01 billion in sales. Plans to split into two companies.
data.description: International Paper Company (NYSE: IP) stock, despite initially rising, is now trading lower due to mixed earnings and a new strategic direction. The company has announced its plan to split into two public entities. In its recent earnings report, IP recorded a fourth-quarter loss of eight cents per share, falling short of the expected 24 cents per share. However, the sales for the quarter stood at $6.01 billion, surpassing the estimated $5.93 billion. Chairman and CEO, Andy Silvernail, noted that the North American sector outperformed the market in the latter part of the year, achieving a 37% year-over-year adjusted EBITDA growth. In EMEA, strategic decisions were made to propel growth. This shift in focus and the earnings variance have influenced the bearish pressure on International Paper stock. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Sanofi's stock rises after Dupixent beats Q4 expectations, an immunological powerhouse. #Investors #Sanofi #Dupixent
data.description: The stock of Sanofi saw a slight increase on Thursday following the strong performance of its immunological drug Dupixent, which exceeded Wall Street's expectations for the fourth quarter. The success of Dupixent, an important asset for Sanofi, contributed to this positive outcome.

data.title: IBM stock surges post Q4 results beat, driven by impressive software and AI growth, outperforming expectations.
data.description: Shares of IBM surged on Thursday following the company's announcement of fourth-quarter financial results that surpassed expectations. The stock saw an increase after reporting strong growth in software and artificial intelligence (AI). The impressive performance resulted in a significant jump in IBM stock.

data.title: UVDI launches V-MIX upper-room air disinfection tech for commercial use, showcased at AHR Expo in Las Vegas.
data.description: In Valencia, California, Ultraviolet Devices Inc. (UVDI) has unveiled its latest V-MIX upper-room air disinfection technology at the AHR Expo in Las Vegas. This innovative technology is designed for various commercial structures such as airports, schools, office buildings, and healthcare facilities. By utilizing a focused, horizontal UV-C light beam, V-MIX effectively deactivates airborne pathogens within the room. This new technology aims to enhance air quality and safety in indoor spaces.

data.title: Discover Puerto Rico showcases island culture through music, food, and storytelling at NYC event during football's biggest weekend.
data.description: In Puerto Rico on January 29, 2026, a showcase was held featuring music-forward activations and a consumer experience in NYC to highlight the cultural influence of Puerto Rico on a large scale. This event coincided with football's peak viewership, attracting a significant global audience. The showcase aimed to integrate various elements to showcase Puerto Rico's culture, demonstrating its impact at a mass level.

data.title: Inventor from Hopkins, Minn. invents heated toilet seat cover for comfort and warmth, portable design.
data.description: In Pittsburgh, on January 29, 2026, an inventor from Hopkins, Minn., shared their inspiration for creating a heated toilet seat cover. The inventor expressed the discomfort of cold and hard toilet seats and aimed to offer comfort and warmth during restroom use. The innovative invention, the HEATED TOILET SEAT COVER, features a portable design that can be conveniently used at home.

data.title: Inaugural DEX ETF Summit in São Paulo aims to develop Brazil's domestic ETF ecosystem for investors and advisors.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, São Paulo will host the first-ever DEX ETF Summit, gathering top figures from Brazil's financial sector to advance the establishment of a contemporary ETF market tailored to local investors and advisors. This event aims to boost the growth of a domestic ETF platform in Brazil.
